question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled() / setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publicly mutable setEnabled(false) path triggered by the DEMO network branch in downloadNextBatch() to permanently halt the downloader mid-stream, causing subsequent transaction records to be silently skipped and the mirror node's transaction history to appear truncated at an attacker-chosen point?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file whose filename lexically matches the DEMO network empty-bucket condition, causing setEnabled(false) to be invoked prematurely and permanently suppressing ingestion of all subsequent transaction history without any operator alert?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled() / setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endDate check in verifySignatures() that calls setEnabled(false) by injecting a stream file with a spoofed future timestamp, causing the downloader to disable itself and omit all real transactions after that point, reorganizing the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency-driven polling loop by flooding the stream source with a high volume of malformed signature files at each poll interval, causing the downloader to exhaust its batch window on invalid files and never advance past a chosen point in transaction history, effectively freezing the visible ledger state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user upload stream files with a mismatched StreamType label into the cloud bucket, causing the downloader to associate RECORD-type signatures with BALANCE-type data files, resulting in hash verification passing on wrong content and transaction history being silently reorganized?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / consensusRatio] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfigured consensusRatio of 0 in CommonDownloaderProperties (reachable via environment variable injection in containerized deployments) to bypass the consensus check entirely in ConsensusValidatorImpl, allowing a single attacker-controlled node signature to cause the downloader to accept a fabricated stream file and reorganize transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / downloadRatio] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a downloadRatio set to the minimum valid value (equal to consensusRatio) so that partialCollection() queries only the minimum number of nodes, reducing the diversity of signatures collected and making it easier for a minority of colluding nodes to present a reorganized transaction history that passes consensus validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / pathPrefix] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the pathPrefix configuration value redirect the S3StreamFileProvider's bucket path to an attacker-controlled prefix, causing the downloader to fetch stream files from attacker-supplied storage and ingest a reorganized transaction history that passes signature verification if the attacker also controls the corresponding node keys?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / endpointOverride] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the endpointOverride configuration value redirect all S3 requests to an attacker-controlled server, serving crafted stream files with valid-looking signatures to reorganize the mirror node's view of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / bucketName] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the bucketName fallback logic in getBucketName() by registering a public cloud bucket with the same name as the default network bucket, causing the downloader to fetch stream files from the attacker's bucket when the legitimate bucket is unavailable, reorganizing transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isWriteFiles() / setWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeFiles flag being enabled to cause the downloader to archive stream files to a shared filesystem path, where the attacker can replace archived files with modified versions that alter the apparent transaction history visible to downstream consumers reading from the archive?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isWriteSignatures() / setWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeSignatures flag to cause signature files from all nodes to be written to a predictable filesystem path, then replace those signature files with crafted ones before a downstream process reads them, causing the apparent consensus to reflect a reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isPersistBytes() / setPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the persistBytes flag being enabled to keep raw stream file bytes in memory across the processing pipeline, then exploit a memory disclosure vulnerability to read and reconstruct the internal state of lastStreamFile, enabling a replay attack that reinserts old transaction history at a later point in the sequence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / batchSize] Can an unprivileged external user flood the stream source with exactly batchSize-1 malformed signature files per poll cycle, ensuring the legitimate next file is always pushed out of the batch window, causing the downloader to perpetually skip the real next transaction file and stall transaction history at an attacker-chosen point?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / maxSize] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that is exactly 1 byte over the maxSize limit and inject it into the stream source ahead of the legitimate file, causing the legitimate file to be rejected by the size check in S3StreamFileProvider and the downloader to skip that transaction batch, creating a gap in transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / timeout] Can an unprivileged external user perform a slow-loris-style attack against the stream source endpoint to cause the downloader's timeout to expire during signature file collection, resulting in partial signature sets that fail consensus and cause the downloader to skip entire batches of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / pathType AUTO] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AUTO pathType transition logic in S3StreamFileProvider, where the path permanently switches to NODE_ID after a successful find, by injecting a single valid-looking NODE_ID-path file to trigger the permanent switch, then serving all subsequent files from an attacker-controlled NODE_ID path that contains a reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / pathRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathRefreshInterval in AUTO pathType mode by timing attacks to coincide with the refresh window, repeatedly causing the path to oscillate between ACCOUNT_ID and NODE_ID, causing the downloader to fetch files from inconsistent sources and ingest a non-sequential transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user upload a file with a valid RECORD stream filename but BALANCE stream content into the node's bucket path, exploiting the fact that getStreamType() is determined by the DownloaderProperties implementation rather than file content, causing the wrong parser to process the file and produce a corrupted transaction history entry?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shouldDownload() check that solely relies on isEnabled() by finding a race condition between the setEnabled(false) call in the endDate branch and the next poll cycle, causing the downloader to process one extra batch of attacker-supplied files after the intended cutoff, appending fabricated transactions to history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / consensusRatio] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the boundary condition where consensusRatio is set to exactly 0 in ConsensusValidatorImpl, which skips the consensus check entirely when signatureHashMap is non-empty, by injecting a single signature file from any source to make the downloader accept any stream file content as valid, reorganizing transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / sources] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sources list in CommonDownloaderProperties by injecting a malicious StreamSourceProperties entry (via configuration injection) that points to an attacker-controlled cloud provider, causing the CompositeStreamFileProvider to fetch stream files from the attacker's source and ingest a reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / allowAnonymousAccess] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the allowAnonymousAccess flag being true for public networks to write attacker-controlled stream files to the public bucket (if the bucket has write permissions), causing the downloader to ingest fabricated transaction history that passes signature verification if the attacker also controls node keys?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / downloadRatio + partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the random shuffle in partialCollection() combined with a low downloadRatio to increase the probability that only attacker-influenced nodes are selected for signature collection in a given poll cycle, allowing a reorganized transaction history to pass consensus with fewer legitimate node signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled() / setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled() has no access control and is called from within the download loop itself, by crafting a sequence of stream files that triggers the endDate disable condition, then re-enabling the downloader via an unauthenticated configuration endpoint to resume ingestion from an attacker-chosen point, skipping intermediate transaction history?
